tagline: Where the Siang becomes the Brahmaputra
description: Pasighat is the oldest town in Arunachal Pradesh and sits where the Siang River exits the mountains and becomes the Brahmaputra. The Adi tribe's homeland. Good base for river activities and for reaching Mechuka.
incitetales_angle: The Siang at Pasighat, where a mountain river emerges from the gorge into the plains, is one of those sights that changes something in you.

destinations.name: Pasighat
destinations.tagline: The Siang emerging from the Himalayan gorge
destinations.description: Pasighat is a proper town with infrastructure — hotels, ATMs, fuel. The Siang River here is powerful. The Adi tribal culture is strong. Used as a base for Mechuka drives or Siang river activities.
what_is_there: Siang River — watch from the banks where the mountain river enters the plains
Daying Ering Wildlife Sanctuary — 190 sq km, gangetic dolphins, river birds
Pasighat town market — good for Adi handicrafts
local_food: Adi pork dishes — smoked, bamboo shoot, various preparations
local_tips: ATM available — withdraw before heading to Mechuka
Pasighat airport operates occasionally — check IndiGo schedules
Dawn walk along the Siang bank is excellent — watch the mist clear

from_guwahati.notes: Long day. Option: fly Guwahati → Pasighat (when IndiGo operates). Pasighat is a gateway for Mechuka.
